2604.09041.title: U-Cast: A Surprisingly Simple and Efficient Frontier Probabilistic AI Weather Forecaster
2604.09041.paper_summary: AI-based weather forecasting now rivals traditional physics-based ensembles, but state-of-the-art (SOTA) models rely on specialized architectures and massive computational budgets, creating a high barrier to entry. We demonstrate that such complexity is unnecessary for frontier performance. We introduce U-Cast, a probabilistic forecaster built on a standard U-Net backbone trained with a simple recipe: deterministic pre-training on Mean Absolute Error followed by short probabilistic fine-tuning on the Continuous Ranked Probability Score (CRPS) using Monte Carlo Dropout for stochasticity. As a result, our model matches or exceeds the probabilistic skill of GenCast and IFS ENS at 1.5$^\circ\$ resolution while reducing training compute by over 10$\times$ compared to leading CRPS-based models and inference latency by over 10$\times$ compared to diffusion-based models. U-Cast trains in under 12 H200 GPU-days and generates a 60-step ensemble forecast in 11 seconds. These results suggest that scalable, general-purpose architectures paired with efficient training curricula can match complex domain-specific designs at a fraction of the cost, opening the training of frontier probabilistic weather models to the broader community. Our code is available at: https://github.com/Rose-STL-Lab/u-cast.
2604.09041.paper_summary_zh: AI 基於人工智慧的天氣預測現在已經可以與傳統的物理基礎集合相媲美，但最先進的 (SOTA) 模型依賴於專門的架構和龐大的計算預算，造成了高進入門檻。<lb/>我們展示了這種複雜性對於前沿性能來說是多餘的。<lb/>我們介紹了 U-Cast，一個基於標準 U-Net 主幹的概率預測器，使用一個簡單的配方進行訓練：在平均絕對誤差上進行確定性預訓練，然後在連續排名概率分數 (CRPS) 上進行短期概率微調，使用蒙地卡羅隨機失活來增加隨機性。<lb/>因此，我們的模型在 1.5$^\circ\$ 分辨率下匹配或超過了 GenCast 和 IFS ENS 的概率技能，同時將訓練計算需求降低了超過 10$\times$，與領先的基於 CRPS 的模型相比，推理延遲降低了超過 10$\times$。<lb/>U-Cast 在不到 12 H200 GPU 天的時間內訓練完成，並在 11 秒內生成 60 步的集合預測。<lb/>這些結果表明，可擴展的通用架構搭配高效的訓練課程可以以更低的成本匹配複雜的領域特定設計，從而使前沿概率天氣模型的訓練對更廣泛的社群開放。<lb/>我們的代碼可在以下網址獲得：https://github.com/Rose-STL-Lab/u-cast。
2604.09041.author: Salva Rühling Cachay et.al.
2604.09041.authors: Salva Rühling Cachay, Duncan Watson-Parris, Rose Yu
